blurb:
When Morrie showed up at Rescue Me GA last fall, he was in rough shape. Extremely skinny with a bad skin infection, he needed months of care in his foster home before he was ready for adoption. It's hard to even guess how old he really is when a dog comes from that kind of neglect, but they think he's somewhere between nine and ten.

Now he's up to a healthy 18 pounds and has turned into this spunky, goofy little Italian Greyhound who keeps his foster mom laughing. Despite everything he's been through, he's got this hilarious personality that just bubbles up once he feels safe. He gets along great with other dogs and seems to enjoy having friends around.

Morrie's looking for his very own person to show him what it's like to be a beloved family member. He's probably never had that before, so he's got a lot of love stored up and ready to give to the right person. After years of neglect, he deserves someone who'll make him the center of their world.

Rescue Me GA works through foster homes for adoptions. Email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an application at www.rmgdogs.org if you think you might be Morrie's person.
scoring_notes:
• Senior dog with months of recovery creates urgent timeline and deep emotional appeal
• Dramatic transformation from neglected to spunky provides inspiring hope narrative
• First-time beloved pet status at his age tugs heartstrings and motivates immediate action

blurb:
Patriot is basically Good Mews' mascot, and once you meet him you'll understand why. He's the kind of cat who jumps in your lap the second you sit down and acts like he's known you his whole life. Super outgoing, loves attention from everyone, and plays with the more active cats at the shelter.

His story is tough but he's made the best of it. He was adopted as a kitten, got hit by a car when his family let him outside, and came back with some medical issues from the accident. He has urine retention and fecal incontinence, which means he needs someone who can handle accidents around the house and stay on top of his medical care.

Here's the thing though: he doesn't feel sorry for himself, so you don't need to either. He genuinely loves living at the shelter because he gets constant attention, but he'd love having his own person even more. If you're up for a cat with some extra needs and a huge personality, he's going to be incredibly grateful.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. Go visit and ask to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Shelter mascot status indicates very long stay and desperate need for home
• Heartwarming story of accident survivor with incredible spirit
• Special needs require dedicated adopter but personality shines through

blurb:
Pork Tenderloin has the best name and he knows it. This handsome guy came from a county shelter for a better shot at finding the right person, and he's pretty clear about what he's looking for: someone who gets cats.

He likes attention, but on his own terms. He'll come find you when he wants affection, which is actually the best kind of cat relationship. No guessing games, no forcing it. When he's in the mood, you'll know. He can get overwhelmed more quickly than some cats, so he needs someone who reads feline body language and respects his boundaries.

This isn't a cat for someone who wants a lap warmer 24/7. This is a cat for someone who appreciates that the best relationships are built on mutual respect. He'll form a real bond with the right person, but you have to earn it first. If you're cat-savvy and patient, he has the potential to be something special.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. Visit and ask to meet him, but let him set the pace.
scoring_notes:
• Memorable name creates instant reader interest and shareability
• Appeals to experienced cat owners seeking meaningful bond
• Requires patient adopter which may limit immediate appeal

blurb:
Panda's got a story. She and her eight puppies were held as part of a court case for a while before Rescue Me GA could get them. Based on the scars she carries, her past wasn't great. But that's all behind her now. She just finished treatment for heartworm disease and is officially ready to move forward.

She's about seven years old but acts like she's half that age. In her foster home, she keeps up with dogs way younger than her, running around and playing like she's making up for lost time. She's not a fan of small dogs, but she loves big dogs who can match her energy. She's great with people and would love nothing more than a comfortable couch to claim as her own.

Panda's looking for someone who can show her what a good life really looks like. She's got plenty of years left to enjoy it, and she's ready to be someone's devoted companion. After everything she's been through, she deserves a family who'll spoil her a little.

Rescue Me GA handles adoptions through foster homes. Email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an application at www.rmgdogs.org to meet her.
scoring_notes:
• Powerful rescue story with court case and healing journey creates strong reader investment
• Transformation from abuse survivor to playful companion offers redemption narrative
• Ready for adoption after medical treatment shows she deserves happy ending urgently

blurb:
Otis was on the county shelter's kill list, not because he was a bad dog, but because he fell in the wrong date range when they were euthanizing for space. When the rescue team found him, he was so stressed in the overcrowded, loud environment that he was urinating on himself. The second they got him into a quiet room, he buried his head in someone's lap and calmed right down. He just needed out.

Now at Our Pals Place, Otis has turned into exactly who he always was underneath all that stress. He's a fun-loving guy who adores attention and wants nothing more than to please his human friends. He quickly remembered house-training and polite sitting once he had space to breathe. The tail-wagging came back too.

Whoever adopts Otis is getting a loyal, loving companion who knows what it's like to be saved. He's not going to take a good home for granted. Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can find their contact information and adoption process on their website or Petfinder page.
scoring_notes:
• Death row rescue story creates powerful emotional hook for readers
• Transformation narrative shows he's ready and grateful for adoption
• Well-trained and loyal companion appeals to wide audience

blurb:
Chocolate Orange is a young female cat at DeKalb County Animal Services who doesn't have a proper bio yet, so we're making one up based on her adorable photos. Look at that face - she's clearly the type of cat who's going to follow you around the house just to see what you're up to. With a name like Chocolate Orange, she's probably got some beautiful tortoiseshell or calico coloring that changes in different light.

She's young, so she's likely got energy to burn and would love interactive toys, climbing trees, or a buddy to wrestle around with. Young cats like her usually adapt quickly to new homes and are great at entertaining themselves when you're busy. She's probably the kind of cat who'll claim your favorite chair as her own and give you that look that says "this is mine now" when you try to sit down.

Since her listing doesn't have much detail, you'll want to visit her in person to see her personality shine through. Young cats are usually pretty adaptable and social, so she's likely to warm up quickly once she meets you. Sometimes the best pets are the ones whose stories you get to discover yourself.

Contact DeKalb County Animal Services to meet Chocolate Orange and see if she's the one.
scoring_notes:
• Young adaptable cat with appealing name and mystery factor attracts wide audience
• County shelter location suggests higher urgency than private rescue placements
• Lack of detailed bio creates blank slate appeal for readers wanting to discover personality

blurb:
Harvey showed up at Rescue Me GA along with his two buddies when their owner passed away. One of them had originally been adopted from RMG as a puppy, so the rescue took in all three dogs together. Nobody gets left behind, right? Harvey's definitely the most chill of the bunch, just rolling with whatever comes his way.

He's got these short little legs on a low-rider body that make him look like he's cruising in slow motion everywhere he goes. The gray around his face makes him look distinguished, but don't let that fool you – he's only about three years old. He gets along great with dogs and has lived with cats before, so he's pretty adaptable.

Nobody knows what breed mix he is, and honestly, it doesn't matter. Sometimes the best dogs are the ones that are just purely themselves. Harvey's looking for someone who appreciates a laid-back companion who's already figured out how to get along with everyone.

Rescue Me GA handles adoptions through their foster network. Email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an application on their website at www.rmgdogs.org to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Owner-surrender due to death creates immediate emotional connection and sympathy
• Easy-going personality with multi-pet compatibility appeals to broad adopter base
• Distinguished appearance with young age offers best of both worlds for families

blurb:
Eugene is a 7-year-old cat who takes his sweet time getting to know you, but once he does, you'll have a loyal companion who purrs so loudly you'll hear it across the room. He's had two failed apartment adoptions because he needs space to observe new people from a distance before he works up the courage to approach. When he's ready, he'll walk in large circles around you, slowly closing the distance until he lets you pet him.

Once Eugene decides you're his person, he'll brush up against you for affection and make biscuits in your lap. You'll know he's completely relaxed when his little tongue sticks out during petting sessions. He's not a fan of being picked up randomly - he much prefers to seek out attention on his own terms. When he wants lap time, he'll brush against your leg and purr, and that's your cue to scoop him up.

Eugene needs to live with another cat or dog. He likes having a buddy around for support and comfort, and he'll sometimes chase after a cat to play or groom them. He's not ideal for apartments - he needs room to roam and observe. If you've got space and patience, Eugene will eventually become the most devoted companion.

Fill out an adoption application at threepawsrescue.org to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Two failed adoptions indicate urgent need for right match to prevent further setbacks
• Compelling transformation story from shy observer to devoted lap cat creates emotional connection
• Specific housing requirements limit pool but story builds empathy for patient adopters

blurb:
Junior's family suddenly became allergic to him after years in their home. At least that's the reason they gave when they dropped him at the county shelter. He got passed by again and again, sitting there scared and depressed. Black dog syndrome is real, and Junior lived it.

But here's what makes Junior special: he absolutely loves ice cream outings. Bruster's and Pelicans are two of his favorite hangouts. After ice cream, he goes home with his favorite volunteer, Katie, and he's a completely different dog. He's well-mannered, house-trained, and loving. He does awesome in a home setting, which is where he belongs.

Junior prefers to be the only dog and does best with older kids. If you're looking for a loyal companion who knows how good it feels to be chosen after being overlooked, Junior is going to be your best buddy. Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can find their contact information and adoption process on their website or Petfinder page.
scoring_notes:
• Black dog syndrome angle highlights important adoption issue
• Unique ice cream dates create memorable and shareable story
• Long shelter stay after family abandonment creates reader urgency

blurb:
Luke's story starts with tragedy but gets better fast. His mom, Miss Daisy, was a gorgeous shepherd whose owner unexpectedly passed away. The boyfriend wouldn't help with her and her two puppies, so he dumped all three at the county shelter. The county held them for weeks hoping family would step up. Nobody did.

Once Our Pals Place rescued them, Miss Daisy could finally relax and Luke could be a puppy. He arrived right during their youth summer camp, so he got tons of attention and walks on the trails. Luke is a fun-loving, playful boy who's on the smaller side for a shepherd. His face looks like a Malinois while his mom looks like a German Shepherd.

Regardless of the breed mix, Luke is a loving boy looking for his forever home. He's young and ready to bond with a family who won't give up on him like the last one did. Our Pals Place is in Marietta. You can find their contact information and adoption process on their website or Petfinder page.
scoring_notes:
• Tragic family loss story creates strong emotional connection
• Young puppy with good socialization appeals to many families
• Shepherd mix offers attractive breed appeal for dog lovers

blurb:
Beignet's got one of those stories that makes you root for her. She was adopted from Good Mews as a kitten, somehow ended up scared and alone at a county shelter, and now she's back where she started looking for round two. Nobody knows what happened in between, but that's behind her now.

She went straight into foster care to get her confidence back, and it worked. She loves being petted and is a social eater who likes company at mealtime. When she wants attention, she'll sit close or lean against you. She can give gentle love bites when she gets overstimulated, so you'll want to watch her body language and give her space when she needs it.

Beignet knows what it's like to lose a home, so she's going to appreciate the right person even more this time around. She's ready for her second chance with someone who understands that she's been through some stuff but came out sweet on the other side.

Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet her.
scoring_notes:
• Compelling comeback story of resilience that readers will connect with emotionally
• Well-socialized cat ready for adoption with clear personality traits
• Second-chance narrative creates urgency and reader investment

blurb:
Brie is a 5-year-old orange-and-white beauty who's carrying a few extra pounds and looking for someone patient enough to help her slim down slowly. She's about 14.5 lbs right now and does best with measured, scheduled meals instead of free-feeding. Don't let her size fool you though - she's got the softest purrs and melts into a puddle of happiness when you brush her head and neck.

She's the kind of cat who'll park herself next to your laptop while you work and politely request pets. She's a little shy at first, but brush time is her love language. Give her a gentle brushing session and you'll have a friend for life. She gets along fine with dogs as long as they give her space, but she's not a fan of other cats - she'll hiss or retreat if one gets too close.

Brie would thrive in a quieter home with adults or calm older teens. High energy overwhelms her, so she's looking for a peaceful place where she can blossom at her own pace. If you're ready to cheer on a sweetheart as she finds her confidence, Brie is waiting for you.

Fill out an adoption application at threepawsrescue.org to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Sweet personality with specific needs makes her relatable to patient adopters
• Weight management story appeals to readers who understand lifestyle changes
• Needs quiet home which may limit adoption pool but creates targeted appeal
